description_long: This is a modern military attack helicopter with a streamlined, angular fuselage painted in a matte gray with yellow accent markings. The helicopter features a large, bubble-shaped cockpit with multiple transparent windows, providing wide visibility for the crew. Its main rotor consists of two long, slightly curved blades with yellow tips, and a smaller tail rotor is mounted on the vertical stabilizer at the rear. The helicopter is equipped with weaponry, including a prominent nose-mounted cannon and missile pods attached to short wing stubs on either side. The landing gear consists of robust skids with angled supports, designed for stability on rough terrain. Additional details include various sensors, antennas, and surface paneling, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance.
description: A gray and yellow military attack helicopter with a bubble cockpit, two-blade main rotor, tail rotor, weaponry, and landing skids.
description_view_0: Side view showing the helicopter's streamlined body, cockpit, main rotor, tail rotor, and weapon pods.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full span of the main rotor blades, tail boom, and symmetrical weapon placement.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the cockpit, main rotor, tail rotor, and landing skids with visible weaponry.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the bubble cockpit, nose cannon, missile pods, and the width of the main rotor.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, slightly curved main rotor blades made of composite material, primarily black with yellow tips, each spanning approximately 6-7 meters with no visible markings except the color contrast.
attribute_tail_rotor: A small, two-blade tail rotor mounted vertically on the left side of the tail fin, colored black with yellow tips, partially protected by the tail structure.
attribute_landing_skids: Pair of robust, angular landing skids made of metal, painted gray with visible crossbars and angled supports for stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple large, angular cockpit windows forming a bubble canopy, made of tinted glass with black framing, providing wide visibility and no visible markings.
